title: Spermatic cord
body_html:
The spermatic cord is the structure that passes through the inguinal canal in males. Its contents are therefore broadly the same as the contents of the inguinal canal (in males).

StructureContents of The Spermatic Cord
Nerves
  • 3 Nerves*
  • Genital branch of genitofemoral, ilioinguinal*, autonomic fibres
Arteries
  • 3 Arteries
  • Artery to Vas Deferens, Cremasteric artery, testicular artery
Fascia
  • 3 Fascial Layers
  • external spermatic, cremasteric, internal spermatic
Other Things
  • 3 Other things
  • Pampiniform plexus, lymphatics, vas deferens

Note ilioinguinal passes through the superficial ring rather than truly through the canal and runs on the outside of the spermatic cord rather than being truly part of it.

Overview of the spermatic cord

Image sourced from Wikipedia
Diagram illustrating layers covering the spermatic cord
